claim: The YOLO demand router schema already implements blocking logic that prevents PRD readiness when evidence agents report missing, clarify, block, or failed status.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues function converts negative evidence agent states into PRD readiness blockers
evidence.why: Core blocking logic that validates the claim by showing exactly how agent results create blockers.

evidence.summary: Executable test case demonstrating that missing/clarify recommendations produce blockers and prd_ready: false
evidence.why: Test proof that the blocking logic actually works as claimed in runtime behavior.

evidence.summary: prd_ready computed as blockers.length === 0, ensuring any evidence agent blocker prevents readiness
evidence.why: Final integration point confirming blockers control PRD readiness.

result.notes: Verification confirms both explorer and cross-checker provided complete evidence without presenting assumptions as facts. All evidence paths reference valid project files (src/demand/router.ts, __tests__/demand-router.test.ts, src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts). The claim is directly supported by code at lines 481-511 (blocking logic), 740 (prd_ready calculation), and test proof at lines 246-286. No unresolved missing data exists, and all blockers/assumptions are properly reflected in the results. The demand acceptance criteria are already met by existing implementation.
